summary:
Claim: The primary site of lesion in rheumatic heart disease (RHD) endocarditis is along the lines of valve closure.  
Evidence: - Lesions develop at sites exposed to high mechanical stress during valve cycling, such as commissural junctions. - Immune-mediated inflammation targets fibrous tissues under repeated shear force, leading to fibrosis and structural damage.  
Answer: A  

Why D is wrong: Free margins are not subject to significant mechanical stress and thus do not show prominent pathological change in RHD.  

(A = Along line of closure of valves)
